title: penstemon floridus subsp. austini
intro: Penstemon floridus subsp. austini, also known as Austin's beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Penstemon floridus subsp. austini include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and can also be used in salves and ointments for topical application.
